DTR  |  Listen to a Classroom Conversation
LCC-HS10-L4-1400P-HSWH-PCI-AAD-01 • HS Social Studies: World History • Industrial Revolution Literary Response (Blake, Wordsworth, Dickens, Douglass)

The Fourth Voice

Click  🎧  to listen.
Listen to a conversation between a girl and a boy. Listen carefully. You will hear the conversation only once. After listening, you will answer some questions.
Listen to the Conversation
1QUESTION 1: What does the girl ask the boy to do?
2QUESTION 2: Why does the boy say Douglass changes Blake?
3QUESTION 3: Why does the girl want Douglass in the closing segment instead of the middle?
Choose the best answer for each question. You may change your answers before submitting.
Answer all 3 questions to submit.
SMART SUPPORT:
Go back to the conversation. Construct a full justification across all three answers. Name what the girl requests, what makes the boy's source different from the others, and where she wants it placed. For each, cite the specific evidence in the dialogue and explain the reasoning that links the evidence to the answer.
CA ELD PI.A.1, PI.A.3, PI.B.5  |  CA HSS 10.3.7  |  HS Social Studies: World History: Industrial Revolution Literary Response (Blake, Wordsworth, Dickens, Douglass)
